steps.title: Translate no real solutions into an inequality
steps.content:
The question wants values of $b$ that make the quadratic have no real solutions. For a quadratic, that happens when the graph misses the $x$-axis, which means the discriminant is negative. For
$$-x^2+bx-49=0$$
the coefficients are $a=-1$, $b=b$, and $c=-49$.

steps.title: Check the boundary integers
steps.content: Since $196=14^2$, the condition is $|b|\lt14$, or $-14\lt b\lt14$. Because $b$ must be an integer, the least possible value is $-13$.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: The least possible integer value of $b$ is -13.
